finding.summary: Kick is at unity gain with true peak at -0.1 dBFS and sample peak at -0.4 dBFS, leaving essentially no headroom for downstream bus or master processing. Spectrum shows a typical kick shape (100 Hz dominant at -18 dB, with a smooth ~-6 dB per octave rolloff through 1 kHz and 4 kHz, no spectral anomalies). Dynamics look healthy: crest factor 12 dB, LRA 8 LU, RMS -10.5 dB. Mono track, empty FX chain, no sends. Stereo metrics are null, consistent with a mono kick at pan 0.
finding.probable_cause: The track is rendered hot relative to a downstream-processing-tolerant staging target. The 0.3 dB gap between sample peak and true peak indicates inter-sample content typical of transient-heavy material (kick) that has not yet passed through a bus limiter.
finding.confidence_reason: The close-to-0 dBFS true peak is directly measurable; whether this is actually a problem depends on the downstream bus/master chain (which is out of scope for a single-track diagnosis). The spectrum data is sparse (only three 1/3-octave bands reported), so detailed tonal conclusions are limited.

evidence_refs.description: -0.1 dBFS true peak; 0.3 dB above sample peak indicates inter-sample content
-10.5 dB RMS across ~9.5 s of signal (silence_fraction 0.05)
12 dB crest factor consistent with healthy kick transients
8 LU LRA — moderate, normal for a kick in isolation
0.05 — capture is mostly signal, level stats are reliable
100 Hz -18 / 1 kHz -24 / 4 kHz -30: smooth -6 dB/octave rolloff, no peaks or dips to flag
0.0 dB — unity gain, no attenuation in place
Empty — track reaches the output unprocessed

proposal.reason: Trim the kick by ~1 dB to leave headroom for downstream bus compression and/or master limiting without risking 0 dBTP overs. The track currently rides at -0.1 dBTP with nothing in its FX chain, which is hot for staging. A 1 dB cut barely changes perceived loudness but moves true peak to roughly -1.1 dBTP.
